lessons.title: While Loops
lessons.concept_intro: A while loop repeats code as long as a condition is True. It's like saying 'keep going until I tell you to stop.'
lessons.analogy: Like eating chips: WHILE there are chips in the bag, keep eating. When the bag is empty, stop.

lessons.title: For Loops & range()
lessons.concept_intro: A for loop repeats code a specific number of times. range(5) gives you 0, 1, 2, 3, 4.
lessons.analogy: Like a coach saying 'do 10 pushups' — you know exactly how many times to repeat.

lessons.title: Break & Continue
lessons.concept_intro: break exits a loop early. continue skips to the next iteration.
lessons.analogy: break = emergency exit. continue = skip this one, move to the next.

lessons.title: Nested Loops
lessons.concept_intro: A loop inside a loop. The inner loop runs completely for each step of the outer loop.
lessons.analogy: Like a clock: the minute hand (inner) goes all the way around for each hour (outer).

lessons.title: Loop Patterns
lessons.concept_intro: Common patterns: counting, accumulating (summing), searching, and filtering.
lessons.analogy: These are the 'recipes' that every programmer uses over and over.

project.title: Number Guessing Game
project.description: Computer picks a random number, player guesses with higher/lower hints.
